method_description: The privacy policy and FAQ link to a native opt-out page at /svc/optout/search/contact_optouts (People Search opt-out). Per the FAQ, the flow is: (1) search by first/last name + state, (2) select matching record, (3) enter a disposable email address + complete CAPTCHA, (4) receive verification email and click confirmation link, (5) record removed within 24-72 hours. The opt-out page is gated behind a Cloudflare Turnstile managed challenge (403), so the actual form could not be loaded or tested. The footer 'Do Not Sell My Personal Information' link points to beenverified.com/app/optout/contact-search (cross-domain, hardcoded in footer HTML). The privacy policy (Section 18.3) also describes a broader CCPA opt-out path via 'our online request form', emailing privacy@peoplesmart.com, or calling/writing — this is separate from the People Search opt-out and may not require email verification.
bot_check: Cloudflare Turnstile managed challenge confirmed on the opt-out page (/svc/optout/search/contact_optouts) — returns 403 with 'Just a moment...' interstitial that never resolves in automated browsers (tested 60+ seconds, 12 retry attempts). This is Cloudflare Turnstile, NOT reCAPTCHA. However, the privacy policy (Section 13.1(d)) states 'Tools such as Google reCAPTCHA and Cloudflare may set cookies to help protect our website from abuse' — confirming both reCAPTCHA and Cloudflare are used on the site. The opt-out form behind the Turnstile gate may use reCAPTCHA (which PIR supports), but this could not be confirmed because Turnstile blocks access to the form. The FAQ also references 'complete the CAPTCHA' in the opt-out flow but does not specify which type.

tier_1.failure_cause: Requires inbox access to complete verification

notes: Primary failure: The FAQ explicitly states the opt-out requires email verification — 'verify your request to opt out by clicking on the link in our verification email'. This requires inbox access and fails Tier 1. Additional concerns: (1) The opt-out page is gated by Cloudflare Turnstile (not reCAPTCHA). The privacy policy Section 13.1(d) confirms the site uses both 'Google reCAPTCHA and Cloudflare' — so the form behind Turnstile may use reCAPTCHA, but this is unconfirmed. (2) The homepage search is B2B-oriented (name + company + job title), not name + city + state. However, the ToS Section 1 describes personal use cases ('reconnect with long-lost friends or relatives, learn about backgrounds of neighbors, online dates'), so PeopleSmart is not exclusively B2B. The People Search likely exists behind the subscriber paywall. (3) The footer 'Do Not Sell My Personal Information' link is hardcoded to beenverified.com/app/optout/contact-search (cross-domain). BeenVerified is NOT mentioned in the ToS or privacy policy. The corporate entity is 'The Lifetime Value Co. LLC acting through PeopleSmart LLC' (privacy policy Section 1.1), owned by 'The Lifetime Value Co. LLC' (Section 17.1). Section 5.3 references 'our group of companies (subsidiaries, ultimate holding company and all its subsidiaries)' but never names them. Footer metadata hints at the relationship: Affiliates link has data-ga-label='Affiliates (BV)', Careers link goes to ltvco.com. (4) The privacy policy Section 18.3 describes an alternative CCPA opt-out path: 'submit a request by filling out our online request form, emailing us at privacy@peoplesmart.com, or contacting us via phone number or postal address'. This broader path may not require email verification but is for CCPA/state law rights, not specifically the People Search opt-out. (5) Only one record can be opted out at a time through the online People Search process — additional records require emailing privacy@PeopleSmart.com. (6) California flow could not be compared on the opt-out page due to Cloudflare block. The privacy policy Section 19 contains a California-specific notice with additional CCPA rights.
